behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is grouping logic itself (keying by trace ID, not service). This can be caught with in-memory inputs to a single correlator function/object; no real infra or model behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the trace-grouping function with mixed-service log entries sharing and not sharing trace IDs, then assert groups are formed strictly by trace ID and include entries from multiple services.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about module handoff (timestamp/skew handling feeding timeline construction), not just a raw sort function. No OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency is stated, so System/Agentic are disqualified by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/correlation/timeline modules together in-process, feed logs from multiple services with known ±500ms skew, and assert final displayed order matches expected request chronology after skew handling.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: The failure can occur at the boundary between percentile/baseline computation and report flag emission. The behavior text is deterministic thresholding/reporting and does not require real OS/network or AI output, which disqualifies System/Agentic here.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline from aggregated latencies through anomaly detector to report generator; provide data where p99 crosses and does not cross 3x baseline, and assert report flags are present/absent accordingly.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is resilience plus state propagation (parser error handling + pipeline continuation + skipped-count reporting), which spans modules. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required by this behavior text, so System is not minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Execute ingest->parse->count/report modules together with a mixed stream of valid and malformed JSON records; assert processing continues, malformed records are excluded, and skipped count matches exactly.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: This is a throughput/backpressure/runtime behavior that depends on real scheduling, buffering, and timing under load; in-process mocks can hide drops/lag. No AI output dependency is present, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real executable/pipeline under load (10,000 entries/sec burst) with real timers and queues, then assert end-to-end processed count equals sent count and processing lag stays within the defined ‘not falling behind’ threshold.
